ddls:
CREATE TABLE "signal_data" (
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal data entry */,
  "status" INTEGER /* Status of the signal (0 or 1) */,
  "clk" INTEGER /* Clock signal indicator */,
  "dout" INTEGER /* Digital output for the signal */,
  "d0" INTEGER /* Input data channel 0 */,
  "d1" INTEGER /* Input data channel 1 */,
  "d2" INTEGER /* Input data channel 2 */,
  "d3" INTEGER /* Input data channel 3 */,
  "d4" INTEGER /* Input data channel 4 */,
  "d5" INTEGER /* Input data channel 5 */,
  "d6" INTEGER /* Input data channel 6 */,
  "d7" INTEGER /* Input data channel 7 */,
  "a0" REAL /* Analog output value */,
  "timestamp" REAL /* Timestamp of the signal data entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the signal data is from */,
  "signal_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal type */,
  "data_frequency" REAL /* Frequency at which the data was sampled */,
  "ml_model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine learning model used for processing the signal */,
  "anomaly_score" REAL /* Anomaly score generated by the machine learning model */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_data_signal_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_type_id") REFERENCES signal_types ("signal_type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_signal_data_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device (e.g., sensor, actuator) */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the device */,
  "status" INTEGER /* Operational status of the device (active/inactive) */,
  "device_model" TEXT /* Model of the device */,
  "manufactured_date" TEXT /* Date the device was manufactured */,
  "warranty_expiration" TEXT /* Warranty expiration date */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance" TEXT /* Date of the next maintenance */,
  "ml_model_id" INTEGER /* ID of the machine learning model used for predictive maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, technician, admin) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time */,
  "last_activity" TEXT /* Last activity time */,
  "is_locked" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is locked */,
  "failed_attempts" INTEGER /* Number of failed login attempts */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., login, data retrieval) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for browser and device information */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the access session in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device that triggered the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., warning, error) */,
  "alert_message" TEXT /* Detailed message describing the alert */,
  "severity" INTEGER /* Severity level of the alert */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the alert */,
  "resolved_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the alert was resolved */,
  "escalation_level" INTEGER /* Escalation level if the alert was not resolved promptly */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device to which the configuration applies */,
  "parameter_name" TEXT /* Name of the parameter being configured */,
  "parameter_value" TEXT /* Value of the configuration parameter */,
  "last_modified" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last modification */,
  "modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the configuration */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_configurations_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "processing_units" (
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processing unit */,
  "unit_name" TEXT /* Name of the processing unit */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the processing unit */,
  "capacity" INTEGER /* Processing capacity of the unit in operations per second */,
  "cpu_usage" REAL /* Current CPU usage */,
  "memory_usage" REAL /* Current memory usage */,
  "last_reboot" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last reboot */,
  "uptime" INTEGER /* Uptime in seconds */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("unit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each system status entry */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the status entry */,
  "system_health" TEXT /* Overall health of the system (e.g., healthy, warning, critical) */,
  "active_connections" INTEGER /* Number of active connections to the system */,
  "cpu_load" REAL /* Current CPU load */,
  "memory_load" REAL /* Current memory load */,
  "disk_usage" REAL /* Current disk usage */,
  "network_traffic" REAL /* Current network traffic */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "signal_types" (
  "signal_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each signal type */,
  "signal_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the signal type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the signal type */,
  "min_value" REAL /* Minimum value for the signal type */,
  "max_value" REAL /* Maximum value for the signal type */,
  "threshold" REAL /* Threshold value for the signal type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("signal_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_processing_jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each processing job */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal being processed */,
  "unit_id" INTEGER /* ID of the processing unit handling the job */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the processing job */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the processing job */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the job (e.g., completed, in progress) */,
  "processing_time" REAL /* Total processing time in seconds */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the job encountered an error */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_processing_jobs_unit_id FOREIGN KEY ("unit_id") REFERENCES processing_units ("unit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_processing_jobs_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signal_data ("signal_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_archives" (
  "archive_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each archived entry */,
  "signal_id" INTEGER /* ID of the signal data being archived */,
  "archive_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was archived */,
  "data" TEXT /* Binary data representing the archived signal data */,
  "archive_size" INTEGER /* Size of the archived data in bytes */,
  "archived_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who archived the data */,
  "restore_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was last restored */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("archive_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_archives_archived_by FOREIGN KEY ("archived_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_archives_signal_id FOREIGN KEY ("signal_id") REFERENCES signal_data ("signal_id")
)
